title: The Bomb Plot Comes Into Focus
description: On pages 2–5, this part focuses on the bomb plot comes into focus, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Bomb Plot Comes Into Focus. Vivi and the others hunt for the hidden cannon before the final explosion can destroy both armies. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: I hate to rain on your parade,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: But since the day I've obtained my Supa Supa devil fruit,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Has even been able to leave a scratch on me.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: No swordsman could possibly defeat me.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: You might want to pack all those fond memories of yours into a pretty little album.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Because while I don't know what kind of swordsmen you've fought in the past...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That you've never gone up against me before.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Hmph, you better hope your swords can cut half as well as your insults.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'll take that as a compliment,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: I wonder how many minutes this fight'll last for...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Oh, I'll finish you off quick, don't you worry.

summary: This part develops the bomb plot comes into focus and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
